prompt:
Security remediation for the Cedar portal. A compatibility profile was applied to both our authentication and session cookies during the last browser rollout. Login still looks healthy in same-site smoke tests, but review found that the shared defaults no longer guarantee secure transport, the external identity-provider return loses its login session under SameSite enforcement, active authentication tickets do not rotate, and the logout deletion header inherits the weakened attributes.

Harden the shared cookie/session defaults while preserving the existing endpoint behavior. Both `__Host-` cookies must be host-only, root-path, HttpOnly, and Secure even when the originating request is HTTP. Use the SameSite policy that permits the existing top-level cross-site GET callback without making either cookie cross-site on subrequests. Keep the 20-minute authentication lifetime and session idle timeout. Active authentication tickets must use sliding renewal, so a request after half the ticket lifetime rotates the protected value. Login must still remember only local return URLs, the callback must redirect to the remembered local path, and logout must clear the authentication cookie with attributes matching the hardened cookie before redirecting.

Run `env DOTNET_CLI_HOME=/tmp/csharp-cookie-hardening-dotnet DOTNET_SKIP_FIRST_TIME_EXPERIENCE=1 DOTNET_CLI_TELEMETRY_OPTOUT=1 DOTNET_NOLOGO=1 Logging__LogLevel__Default=Critical dotnet run --project protected_tests/CookieHardening.ProtectedTests.csproj`. The protected runner uses an injected in-memory server: no listener, external identity provider, package download, or network access is involved. Do not edit the protected test runner, either project file, NuGet.Config, or .gitignore.
verify_cmd: env DOTNET_CLI_HOME=/tmp/csharp-cookie-hardening-dotnet DOTNET_SKIP_FIRST_TIME_EXPERIENCE=1 DOTNET_CLI_TELEMETRY_OPTOUT=1 DOTNET_NOLOGO=1 Logging__LogLevel__Default=Critical dotnet run --project protected_tests/CookieHardening.ProtectedTests.csproj

full_distill.primary_skill: repair shared ASP.NET Core authentication and session cookie policy while preserving cross-site login callbacks, rolling ticket renewal, redirects, and logout semantics
full_distill.baseline_evidence: the protected runner deterministically reports the legacy shared profile as SameAsRequest, SameSite=Strict, and non-sliding; HTTP responses emit non-Secure __Host- cookies, the simulated top-level identity-provider return withholds the Strict session cookie, no authentication renewal is emitted after half-life, and logout deletion inherits the weakened policy
full_distill.success_contract: framework-resolved options and real middleware responses use host-only root-path HttpOnly cookies with SecurePolicy.Always and SameSite=Lax, an external top-level GET callback completes and preserves a local redirect, an active ticket rotates after half of its 20-minute lifetime, and logout emits a matching hardened deletion cookie
adversarial_factors: the transport assertion starts login over HTTP, so relying on the request scheme instead of SecurePolicy.Always cannot satisfy the __Host- contract
the login flow models browser SameSite delivery on a cross-site top-level GET and requires the server-side session state, so a Strict cookie fails even if its header merely looks plausible
the callback and challenge tests pin remembered local redirects and reject external return URLs, preventing security-option changes from weakening redirect validation
a mutable injected TimeProvider advances beyond half of the authentication lifetime and compares protected cookie values, distinguishing actual sliding renewal from a static option-shaped workaround
logout inspects the expired Set-Cookie line for the same root path, Secure, HttpOnly, and SameSite attributes before proving the cleared browser is challenged on the protected endpoint
the host runs through an injected IServer and asserts that no address is published, keeping acceptance offline and rejecting a replacement listener-based test path

notes: Wave 10 chunk 10SR3 security-remediation seed. The sole planted defect is the three-value legacy profile in PortalCookieDefaults.cs: transport is SameAsRequest, flow policy is Strict, and active-ticket rotation is disabled. Both option graphs intentionally consume that one profile. The remainder of the application is correct and protected integration coverage pins secure __Host- attributes, Lax top-level callback semantics, local-only redirect preservation, middleware-issued sliding renewal, and matching logout deletion. The harness is a package-free executable over Microsoft.AspNetCore.App and a capture IServer, with NuGet sources cleared for deterministic offline verification. Reference fix changes only those three shared profile values and never touches tests. Auth/session durations remain 20 minutes.
